question_text: White light from the Sun is split into a rainbow when it passes through a prism. Which of the following is the correct order of colours in a rainbow, from red to violet?

options.label: A
options.text: Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet

options.label: B
options.text: Violet, indigo, blue, green, yellow, orange, red

options.label: D
options.text: Orange, red, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet

explanation: When white light passes through a prism, it splits into the visible spectrum in a specific order. This order is remembered by the acronym ROY G BIV (red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet), where red has the longest wavelength and violet has the shortest.
